name: Sierra de la Puebla Loop
name_original: Berzosa de Lozolla, Puebla de la Sierra, El Cardoso de la Sierra – ruta circular
description: Tackle the difficult Sierra de la Puebla Loop, a 45.1-mile road cycling route with 4840 feet of elevation gain through scenic mountains.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Berzosa Del Lozoya offer varied terrain within the Sierra Norte of Madrid. The region is characterized by mountainous backdrops, including the foothills of the Cerros de la Mujer Muerta, providing panoramic views from an elevation of 1,096 meters. Cyclists will encounter routes alongside the Lozoya and Riato rivers, as well as the El Villar and El Atazar reservoirs. The landscape features diverse vegetation, from scrubland and rockrose to dense oak and pine forests. Best road cycling routes around Berzosa Del Lozoya The most popular road cycling route is Sierra de la Puebla Loop, a 45.1 miles (72.5 km) trail that takes 4 hours 22 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain through the mountainous Sierra Norte. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Berzosa de Lozoya, Puebla de la Sierra, Paredes de Buitrago – circular route, a difficult 33.3 miles (53.6 km) path. This route offers challenging ascents and descents through picturesque towns and varied landscapes. Local road cyclists also love the El Villar Reservoir and Gorge – Ascent to Puerto de La Puebla loop from Prádena del Rincón, a 50.1 miles (80.6 km) trail leading through reservoir areas and towards the Puerto de La Puebla, often completed in about 4 hours 13 minutes. Road cycling around Berzosa Del Lozoya is defined by mountainous terrain, scenic reservoirs, and diverse forests, with routes offering options for different ability levels, from moderate to difficult. The routes in Berzosa Del Lozoya are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 29 reviews. More than 4,900 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Berzosa Del Lozoya's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Berzosa Del Lozoya?
A: There are nearly 40 road cycling routes available around Berzosa Del Lozoya, offering a wide range of options for cyclists. The komoot community has rated these routes highly, with an average score of 4.8 stars from over 29 reviews.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Berzosa Del Lozoya?
A: Road cycling routes in Berzosa Del Lozoya are characterized by mountainous terrain, nestled in the Sierra Norte of Madrid. You'll encounter challenging ascents and descents, with routes winding alongside the Lozoya and Riato rivers, and past significant water bodies like the El Villar and El Atazar reservoirs. The landscape features diverse vegetation, from scrubland and rockrose to dense oak and pine forests, offering varied and scenic backdrops.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes in Berzosa Del Lozoya?
A: Yes, Berzosa Del Lozoya is known for its challenging road cycling routes. Out of the nearly 40 routes, 25 are classified as difficult, featuring significant elevation gains and demanding terrain. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e977461751/sierra-de-la-puebla-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de la Puebla Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 45.1-mile (72.5 km) route with over 1,400 meters of elevation gain. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31528830" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Villar Reservoir and Gorge – Ascent to Puerto de La Puebla loop from Prádena del Rincón&lt;/a&gt;, covering 50.1 miles (80.6 km) with over 1,500 meters of climbing.

Q: Are there any easy road cycling routes for beginners in Berzosa Del Lozoya?
A: While the region is predominantly known for its challenging routes, there is at least one easy road cycling route available. The broader Sierra Norte also offers sections of trails like the Lozoya Valley Nature Trail, which can be suitable for less experienced cyclists or those looking for a more relaxed ride, though it's often geared towards mountain or gravel biking.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes available in Berzosa Del Lozoya?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes in Berzosa Del Lozoya are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. A notable example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e977467751/berzosa-de-lozoya-puebla-de-la-sierra-paredes-de-buitrago-circular-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Berzosa de Lozoya, Puebla de la Sierra, Paredes de Buitrago – circular route&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 33.3-mile (53.6 km) path that offers challenging ascents and descents through picturesque towns. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24407593" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Canencia Pass – Puerto de Canencia climb loop from Puentes Viejas&lt;/a&gt;, a 75.5 km difficult loop.

Q: What natural features will I encounter on road cycling routes in this region?
A: The region offers a rich tapestry of natural features. You'll cycle through areas with scrubland, dense oak and pine forests, and along the banks of the Lozoya and Riato rivers. The routes often provide stunning views of the El Villar and El Atazar reservoirs, Madrid's largest. The mountainous backdrop includes the foothills of the Cerros de la Mujer Muerta, earning Berzosa del Lozoya the nickname 'balcony of the Sierra Norte' for its panoramic vistas.

Q: What landmarks or viewpoints can I see while cycling around Berzosa Del Lozoya?
A: While cycling around Berzosa Del Lozoya, you can enjoy several significant landmarks and viewpoints. You might pass by the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2791690" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Villar Reservoir and Gorge&lt;/a&gt;, or catch views of the expansive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6217974" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Atazar Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. Mountain passes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3080243" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puerto de la Puebla&lt;/a&gt; offer excellent viewpoints, and you might even spot the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3274718" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Berrueco Watchtower&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Is parking available for road cyclists in Berzosa Del Lozoya?
A: Yes, parking is generally available in and around Berzosa Del Lozoya, especially in the town center, which serves as a common starting point for many cycling routes. Given the region's popularity for outdoor activities, you'll find suitable spots to park your vehicle before heading out on your ride.

Q: Can I reach the cycling routes around Berzosa Del Lozoya by public transport?
A: Public transport options to Berzosa Del Lozoya are available, primarily via bus services connecting from Madrid. While direct access to every trailhead might require a short ride from the bus stop, the town itself is a hub for many routes, making it feasible to reach the area without a car. It's advisable to check local bus schedules for the most up-to-date information.

Q: Are there places to stop for refreshments or accommodation along the routes?
A: Yes, the Sierra Norte region, including Berzosa Del Lozoya and its surrounding towns, offers various amenities for cyclists. You'll find cafes, restaurants, and accommodation options in the charming towns that many routes pass through, such as Puebla de la Sierra and Paredes de Buitrago. These spots provide excellent opportunities to refuel and rest during or after your ride.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Berzosa Del Lozoya?
A: The best time for road cycling in Berzosa Del Lozoya is generally during spring (April-June) and autumn (September-October). During these seasons, the weather is typically mild and pleasant, with less extreme temperatures than the hot summer months or the colder winter. The diverse vegetation is also particularly beautiful during these times, enhancing the scenic experience.

Q: Are there family-friendly road cycling routes in Berzosa Del Lozoya?
A: While many routes in Berzosa Del Lozoya are challenging, the broader Lozoya Valley offers sections that are more suitable for families. These might include flatter paths along the reservoirs or dedicated nature trails, which provide a gentler experience away from the demanding mountain ascents. It's recommended to look for routes specifically marked as easy or moderate if cycling with family.
tips: On the Puebla de la Sierra highway you come across this viewpoint over the Puebla river gorge. The landscape is overwhelming. The vertical walls of bare rock stand before the green ridges of the mountains and the sky. It is an ideal place to immortalize with a photograph.
There is usually very little traffic and the road surface is excellent. Be sure to stop at La Hiruela, it is a beautiful town.
The surroundings of this Madrid town are an extraordinary place to go on a bike route, especially during autumn, due to the abundance of deciduous trees.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Sierra de la Puebla Loop?
faqs.answer: This racebike route is rated as difficult. It features continuous ascents and descents with significant elevation gains, totaling approximately 1475 meters over its 72.5-kilometer length. While primarily paved, some segments may be on narrow roads, requiring a good level of fitness and cycling experience.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this racebike route?
faqs.answer: On average, cyclists can expect to complete the Sierra de la Puebla Loop in about 4 hours and 8 minutes. However, this duration can vary based on your fitness level, the number of stops you make, and weather conditions.

faqs.question: What kind of scenery can I expect along the way?
faqs.answer: The route offers a diverse and beautiful experience, winding through well-preserved natural landscapes, mountains, and alongside rivers. You'll pass through charming, traditional villages with authentic architecture, providing a rich cultural backdrop to the stunning natural views.

faqs.question: Are there any notable landmarks or viewpoints on the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route features several points of interest. You'll pass by the scenic Vultures' Viewpoint, cycle over the challenging Puerto de La Hiruela, and go through the traditional settlement of Montejo de la Sierra.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this racebike route?
faqs.answer: The Sierra de la Puebla Loop typically starts and ends near Berzosa de Lozoya. You can usually find parking available in or around the villages along the route, such as Berzosa de Lozoya, Puebla de la Sierra, or El Cardoso de la Sierra, which serve as convenient starting points.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Sierra de la Puebla region is best enjoyed during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder and the natural scenery is particularly vibrant. Summer can be hot, and winter may bring colder temperatures and potential for snow at higher elevations, making cycling more challenging.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or fees required to cycle in the Sierra de la Puebla Biosphere Reserve?
faqs.answer: The Sierra de la Puebla Loop passes through the Sierra del Rincón, which is recognized as a Biosphere Reserve. Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for cycling on public roads within the reserve. However, it's always advisable to respect local regulations, stay on marked paths, and practice leave-no-trace principles to preserve this protected natural area.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on this racebike route?
faqs.answer: While the route is primarily on public roads, which are generally accessible, it's a racebike route not specifically designed for pets. If you bring a dog, ensure it is well-behaved and kept on a leash, especially when passing through villages or near wildlife. Always clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other cycling trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Sierra de la Puebla Loop intersects with several other paths. Notably, it covers significant sections of the [CIMA MA10] La Puebla * La Hiruela and [CIMA MA10] La Puebla * La Puebla de la Sierra. You'll also encounter parts of Senderos del Jarama, GR 300 - Senda del Genaro, and various local paths like Senda de La Fuente Lugar and Senda Los Oficios de la Vida.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or drink along the Sierra de la Puebla Loop?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the route passes through several traditional villages like Montejo de la Sierra, Puebla de la Sierra, and La Hiruela, you will find opportunities to stop for refreshments. These villages often have small cafes, restaurants, or bars where you can refuel and experience local hospitality.

faqs.question: Is this racebike route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Given its 'difficult' rating, significant elevation gain, and varied terrain, this route is not recommended for beginners. It is best suited for experienced road cyclists who are comfortable with long distances, sustained climbs, and descents.
